extracted_information: The provided web content details various Kubernetes autoscaling patterns, including horizontal and vertical scaling for workloads, and scaling based on cluster infrastructure. It covers Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA), Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA), and cluster-size-based autoscalers like Cluster Proportional Autoscaler, along with mentions of event-driven and scheduled autoscaling. The document clarifies the difference between manual and automatic scaling.

HorizontalPodAutoscaler.function: Periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a workload.
HorizontalPodAutoscaler.metrics_supported: Observed resource utilization (e.g., CPU, memory usage).

VerticalPodAutoscaler.availability: Separate project, not default with Kubernetes (found on GitHub).
VerticalPodAutoscaler.function: Allows creation of CustomResourceDefinitions (CRDs) to define how and when to scale resources of managed replicas.

modes.description: Currently `Recreate`. Might change to in-place updates in the future.
Assigns resource requests on pod creation and updates them on existing pods by evicting them when requested resources differ significantly from new recommendation.
Assigns resource requests only on pod creation and never changes them later.
Does not automatically change resource requirements of pods. Recommendations are calculated and can be inspected in the VPA object.

in_place_pod_vertical_scaling.status_as_of_v1.34: VPA does not support resizing pods in-place; integration is being worked on.

ClusterProportionalAutoscaler.availability: Separate project, not part of Kubernetes core (on GitHub).
ClusterProportionalAutoscaler.function: Watches number of schedulable nodes and cores and scales the number of replicas of the target workload accordingly.

ClusterProportionalVerticalAutoscaler.availability: Separate project, not part of Kubernetes core (on GitHub).
ClusterProportionalVerticalAutoscaler.function: Adjusts resource requests for a workload (e.g., Deployment or DaemonSet) based on the number of nodes and/or cores in the cluster, when the number of replicas should stay the same.

KubernetesEventDrivenAutoscaler (KEDA).function: Enables scaling workloads based on the number of events to be processed (e.g., messages in a queue).
KubernetesEventDrivenAutoscaler (KEDA).features: Wide range of adapters for different event sources.

features.description: Increases or decreases the number of replicas managed by the workload.
Adjusts the resources available to the replicas in-place.
features.manual_option: By patching the resource definition of the workload.

features.description: Automatically updates objects that manage a set of Pods (e.g., Deployments).
sub_types: Autoscaling based on schedules (via KEDA's Cron scaler)

features.description: Adds or removes nodes from the cluster.
